title: Fable 007.1: gate full-tool socket startup
description:
## Outcome
`rpce-headless serve` has an explicit, default-off full-tool socket flag guarded by startup token validation.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Fable 007 authenticated full-tool socket mode.
- Source plan steps: 1 and 2.
- Preserves: socket mode remains restricted unless `--expose-all-tools` is present with `--socket`.

## Success criteria
- `HeadlessCLI.Command.serve` carries `exposeAllTools: Bool`.
- `serve` accepts `--expose-all-tools` only for the `serve` subcommand.
- `--expose-all-tools` without `--socket` exits 64 with `--expose-all-tools requires --socket`.
- When `exposeAllTools` is true, startup reads `RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN` once and trims whitespace/newlines.
- Missing `RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN` exits 64 with a clear message containing `RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN` and no token value.
- A token with fewer than 16 trimmed characters exits 64 with a clear message containing `at least 16` and no token value; 16 or more trimmed characters is accepted by this startup gate.
- The accepted trimmed token, or auth configuration derived from it, is carried through serve dispatch/socket startup plumbing for the downstream protocol-auth bead.
- The auth handoff must preserve enough in-process secret material or a token-verification capability for Fable 007.2 while preventing the raw token from appearing in logs, errors, debug output, or closeout evidence.
- Usage shows `serve --root <path> ... [--socket <path> [--expose-all-tools]]`.
- Ordinary `serve --socket` startup without `--expose-all-tools` still works and does not require `RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN`.
- Add focused parser/startup tests if the target has a suitable seam; otherwise record why no seam exists and rely on the downstream socket smoke bead for black-box coverage.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: parser, command enum, serve dispatch plumbing, startup token validation.
- Do: make the accepted auth configuration available to socket startup for later handshake enforcement.
- Do not: implement the socket handshake, bridge auth, schema changes, or docs prose.
- Do not change stdio `serve` full-tool behavior.

## Failure behavior
- Unknown or misplaced `--expose-all-tools` fails through the existing usage path.
- Invalid token configuration fails before the listener accepts connections.
- Token value is never interpolated into errors, logs, debug output, tests, or closeout evidence.
- Positive startup evidence must prove the listener/startup path is reached without printing the accepted token.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- CLI surface: `rpce-headless serve --root ... --socket ... --expose-all-tools`.
- Token env: `RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN`.
- Auth handoff: serve dispatch/socket startup should receive a trimmed-token-backed verifier or redacted auth config for `Fable 007.2`.
- Default socket path: `rpce-headless serve --root ... --socket ...` remains restricted and token-free.
- Likely files:
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/main.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessAgentTypes.swift` for `RPCE_` env style reference.

## Validation
```bash
make dev-swift-build PRODUCT=rpce-headless
.build/debug/rpce-headless serve --root . --expose-all-tools
RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN= .build/debug/rpce-headless serve --root . --socket /tmp/rpce-auth.sock --expose-all-tools
RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN=123456789012345 .build/debug/rpce-headless serve --root . --socket /tmp/rpce-auth-short.sock --expose-all-tools
SOCKET=/tmp/rpce-restricted.sock; LOG=/tmp/rpce-restricted.log; rm -f "$SOCKET" "$LOG"; env -u RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN .build/debug/rpce-headless serve --root . --socket "$SOCKET" 2>"$LOG" & pid=$!; for _ in {1..20}; do [ -S "$SOCKET" ] && break; sleep 0.1; done; test -S "$SOCKET"; kill "$pid"; wait "$pid" || true; rm -f "$SOCKET" "$LOG"
SOCKET=/tmp/rpce-auth-positive.sock; LOG=/tmp/rpce-auth-positive.log; rm -f "$SOCKET" "$LOG"; RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN=1234567890123456 .build/debug/rpce-headless serve --root . --socket "$SOCKET" --expose-all-tools 2>"$LOG" & pid=$!; for _ in {1..20}; do [ -S "$SOCKET" ] && break; sleep 0.1; done; test -S "$SOCKET"; ! grep -q 1234567890123456 "$LOG"; kill "$pid"; wait "$pid" || true; rm -f "$SOCKET" "$LOG"
```
Expected:
- Build exits 0.
- Invalid invocations exit 64 with the planned messages.
- Ordinary restricted `serve --socket` reaches the listener/startup path without `RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN`.
- The positive-token smoke is bounded by the script, reaches the listener/startup path, and does not print the accepted token.
- Stderr/log evidence from invalid-token cases does not include the token value.
- No long-lived socket process remains after invalid-token validation.
- If parser/startup tests are not added, closure evidence explains the missing seam and points to downstream smoke coverage.

Behavior-test intent: add or extend parser/startup coverage if this target has a suitable CLI test seam; otherwise the smoke bead must cover these paths.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- This is the first implementation child and can start immediately.
- Unblocks socket protocol, bridge auth, smoke coverage, and example text.
- Single-owner risk: `main.swift` parser/usage.

## Closure evidence
Close with: commands run, exit codes/messages observed, default restricted socket evidence, positive-token startup evidence, token secrecy check, no lingering listener after validation, parser/startup test or seam rationale, commit SHA or uncommitted note.

title: Fable 007.3: add connect --auth bridge handshake
description:
## Outcome
`rpce-headless connect --socket <path> --auth` performs the auth preamble itself, then exposes only clean MCP JSON-RPC over stdio.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Fable 007 authenticated full-tool socket mode.
- Source plan step: 4.
- Preserves: `connect --socket <path>` without `--auth` behaves as today's raw byte bridge.
- Protocol frames come from Fable 007.2: client sends `{"rpce_auth":{"token":"<value>"}}\n`; accepted response is `{"rpce_auth":{"status":"accepted"}}\n`; rejected response is `{"rpce_auth":{"status":"rejected"}}\n`.

## Success criteria
- `HeadlessCLI.Command.connect` carries `auth: Bool` and parser accepts optional `--auth`.
- With `--auth`, bridge reads `RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN` from the environment.
- Missing or empty token exits 64 with `rpce-headless connect: missing RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN` on stderr and no socket pumps started.
- After connect, bridge writes the auth JSON line and reads exactly one newline-delimited response before starting pumps.
- Rejected, malformed, EOF, socket-close, timeout, or no-response auth exits 65 with `rpce-headless connect: socket authentication rejected` on stderr.
- Accepted response is not forwarded to stdout; MCP pumps start only after auth succeeds.
- Without `--auth`, the byte-pump behavior and restricted-socket compatibility remain unchanged.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: connect parser, auth option plumbing, bridge preamble write, response read, exit behavior.
- Do: add a focused bridge-level proof for accepted auth, missing token, rejected auth, malformed/no-response auth, and unchanged no-auth behavior.
- Do: use a tiny fake Unix-socket responder or equivalent focused harness for malformed and no-response bridge replies, since the real server should not emit malformed auth.
- Do not: implement server auth protocol here, change MCP request framing, or auto-enable auth.
- Do not: take over the downstream full socket-auth matrix, dedicated smoke-script creation, or final integration closeout owned by Fable 007.4.
- Do not print the token value or the raw accepted/rejected auth frame to stdout.

## Failure behavior
- Missing env token: exit 64 with `rpce-headless connect: missing RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN` before sending an auth frame.
- Rejected auth or malformed response: exit 65 and do not pump stdin/stdout.
- EOF, socket close before a newline-delimited auth response, no response, or an unparsable response is malformed auth and exits 65 before pumps start.
- Timeout/no response is bounded by the established smoke harness timeout budget, with a 12-second scenario assertion budget and only small subprocess cleanup grace.
- Timeout/no response evidence must show exit 65 and no stdout JSON-RPC/auth-frame pump before close.
- Socket connect failures keep existing error behavior.
- Plain `connect --socket` remains usable for discovery-restricted sockets with no handshake.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- CLI surface: `rpce-headless connect --socket <path> --auth`.
- Likely files:
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/main.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/ConnectBridge.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/socket_auth_smoke.py`
- Test surface: bridge subprocess stdio against a real or fake Unix socket.

## Validation
```bash
make dev-swift-build PRODUCT=rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/socket_auth_smoke.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
```
Expected for this bead:
- Accepted bridge auth can speak clean JSON-RPC through stdio.
- No `rpce_auth` line appears on stdout.
- Missing/rejected/malformed/no-response bridge auth exits with expected codes/messages.
- Unauthenticated bridge behavior remains unchanged.

Behavior-test intent:
- This bead owns narrow bridge-path proof only: accepted auth, missing token, rejected auth, malformed/no-response auth, and no-auth regression.
- Fable 007.4 owns creating or completing the dedicated end-to-end smoke matrix across startup gate, socket protocol, bridge path, restricted sockets, and stdio regressions.
- Do not close this bead with a discretionary malformed-response gap; prove it with a fake responder/focused harness or keep the bead open.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on CLI startup gate and socket protocol.
- Unblocks final socket smoke completion.
- Single-owner risk: `ConnectBridge.swift` pump startup ordering.
- Smoke ownership boundary: this bead owns bridge-specific cases only; Fable 007.4 owns the full black-box matrix and closeout script completeness.

## Closure evidence
Close with: build result, bridge-scenario proof result, stderr/exit checks for missing/rejected/malformed/no-response cases, no-auth regression note, no-auth-frame-stdout evidence, commit SHA or uncommitted note.

title: Fable 007.5: add deployment breadcrumbs and status closeout
description:
## Outcome
Deployment examples mention the opt-in full-tool socket safely, and the Fable plan index updates only after validation passes.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Fable 007 authenticated full-tool socket mode.
- Source plan step: 6 and done criteria.
- Preserves: plan 012 owns full README docs; this bead only adds breadcrumbs and status closeout.

## Success criteria
- `Examples/rpce-headless.env` adds a commented token block only:
  - required only for `serve --socket --expose-all-tools`.
  - includes `openssl rand -hex 32` generation hint.
  - leaves `RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN=` commented.
- `Examples/rpce-headless.service` adds a commented alternate `ExecStart` above the active one.
- Active service behavior remains restricted by default.
- `docs/plans/fable/README.md` status row for plan 007 updates only after validation passes, using the existing index convention for a completed plan:
  - completed status label;
  - closeout date, set to the date the validation evidence is accepted;
  - evidence pointing to the socket auth smoke/regression outputs.
- Encode the accepted-evidence closeout date and smoke/regression evidence inside the existing status-cell convention and date format.
- Do not add a new README section or change the table schema unless the file already uses that convention.
- Before updating the Fable README status row, use completed Fable 007.4 closure evidence or recorded smoke/regression command output as the source of truth.
- If that evidence is unavailable or failing, leave the status row unchanged and report the missing evidence.
- Final grep checks prove full access and token reads stayed in intended places.
- Changed-file evidence shows only these intended closeout paths for this bead: the env example, service example, and Fable README status row.
- No other docs or README prose are edited for this feature.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: comment-only examples, final plan-index status update, and parent grep evidence.
- Do not: create full docs, alter active systemd command, or include a real token.
- Do not update the status row before implementation and smoke checks complete.

## Failure behavior
- If examples need non-comment changes, stop and report; that is outside this plan.
- If validation or grep evidence is incomplete, leave the status row unchanged.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- Examples:
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Examples/rpce-headless.env`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Examples/rpce-headless.service`
- Status index: `docs/plans/fable/README.md`.
- Security grep surfaces:
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessMCPServer.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/*.swift`

## Validation
```bash
git diff -- Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Examples/
grep -n "RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Examples/rpce-headless.env
grep -n -- "--expose-all-tools" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Examples/rpce-headless.service
rg -n "discoveryRestricted: false" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer
rg -n "RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/*.swift
git diff --name-only
make dev-lint
```
Expected:
- Example diffs are comment-only.
- `discoveryRestricted: false` appears only in the new handler and pre-existing stdio path.
- Token reads never interpolate token values into logs/errors.
- Changed files for this closeout are limited to the two example files and `docs/plans/fable/README.md` status row; explain any additional already-touched implementation files separately.
- Lint exits 0.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on CLI startup gate so final flag/env names are settled.
- Status row update depends on smoke/regression coverage passing.
- Can be implemented separately from protocol code, but should close last.

## Closure evidence
Close with: example diff summary, plan status evidence, changed-file evidence, grep outputs summarized, validation commands, commit SHA or uncommitted note.

title: Fable 007: authenticated full-tool socket mode
description:
## Outcome
Ship `rpce-headless serve --socket --expose-all-tools` as an authenticated, opt-in full-tool Unix socket mode while preserving today's restricted socket defaults.

## Background
- Source plan: `docs/plans/fable/007-full-tool-socket-auth.md`.
- The deployed systemd path uses socket mode, but socket connections currently expose only discovery tools.
- Full agentic tools over an unauthenticated socket would be an arbitrary-process-execution surface.
- Final decision to preserve: full-tool socket transport and shared-token auth land together.
- Internal discovery/callback sockets must remain restricted and unauthenticated.

## Closure contract
Do not close this parent until every direct child is closed or explicitly closed as unnecessary with evidence.

## Children / intended order
1. `repoprompt-ce-fable-007-cli-startup-gate-2mr` / CLI startup gate: parse `--expose-all-tools` and require `RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN`.
2. `repoprompt-ce-fable-007-socket-auth-protocol-l4q` / Socket protocol: add authenticated full-access connection handling without over-reading JSON-RPC bytes.
3. `repoprompt-ce-fable-007-connect-auth-bridge-o7v` / Bridge auth: let `connect --auth` perform the pre-JSON-RPC handshake without leaking auth frames.
4. `repoprompt-ce-fable-007-socket-auth-smoke-2sb` / Smoke coverage: prove happy, rejection, crash-regression, restricted-control, bridge, and stdio regressions.
5. `repoprompt-ce-fable-007-examples-status-closeout-nrn` / Example/status closeout: add commented deployment breadcrumbs and update the plan status row.

## Scope
- In-scope files only:
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/main.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessMCPServer.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/ConnectBridge.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/socket_auth_smoke.py`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Examples/rpce-headless.env`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Examples/rpce-headless.service`
  - `docs/plans/fable/README.md` status row
- Use the coordinated daemon validation commands from `AGENTS.md`.

## Non-goals
- No TCP/TLS transport.
- No `HeadlessToolSchemas.swift` schema changes.
- No README prose beyond the two example config breadcrumbs; plan 012 owns docs.
- No crypto dependency for token comparison.
- No auth path on `HeadlessContextBuilderService` or `HeadlessAgentSessionManager` internal sockets.

## Parent acceptance criteria
- Full-tool socket requires `--expose-all-tools` plus a non-empty token of at least 16 trimmed characters.
- Accepted socket clients can list/use the full tool set, including `agent_run`, `agent_manage`, `context_builder`, and `oracle_send`.
- Rejected, malformed, empty-token, missing-handshake, and timeout clients do not receive tools and do not crash the daemon.
- Restricted socket mode without `--expose-all-tools` remains handshake-free and discovery-only.
- Stdio `serve` behavior remains full-tool and unaffected.
- `connect --auth` hides the auth response from stdout and exits clearly on missing or rejected auth.
- Deployment examples are comment-only breadcrumbs; token values are never logged or printed.

## Validation
```bash
make dev-swift-build PRODUCT=rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/socket_auth_smoke.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/mcp_smoke.py .build/debug/rpce-headless "$PWD"
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/mcp_agent_smoke.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
make dev-format-check
make dev-lint
rg -n "RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN|rpce_auth|discoveryRestricted: false" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer
br dep cycles --json
bv --robot-insights
bv --robot-plan
```
Expected grep evidence:
- Token values are never printed/logged or interpolated into errors.
- `discoveryRestricted: false` appears only in the new full-access socket handler and the pre-existing stdio path.
- Internal discovery/callback socket paths still instantiate restricted tool surfaces.

## Stop conditions
- Stop if `StdioTransport` cannot operate after pre-reading the auth line.
- Stop if live `HeadlessMCPServer.runSocketConnection` no longer matches the expected restricted pattern.
- Stop if implementation seems to require a crypto dependency.
- Stop if the same verification step fails twice after a reasonable fix attempt.

## Parallelization notes
- Treat `HeadlessMCPServer.swift` auth protocol work as single-owner.
- Parser/startup and example breadcrumbs can be reviewed separately from protocol work.
- Smoke coverage should land after parser, protocol, and bridge behavior are available.

## Closure evidence
Close with: child bead IDs closed, validation commands and results, grep checks for token secrecy/restricted sockets, a `socket_auth_smoke.py` note that rejected, malformed, empty-token, missing-handshake, and timeout clients did not expose tools or crash the daemon, commit SHA or note that changes remain uncommitted.

title: Fable 007.2: authenticate full-access socket connections
description:
## Outcome
Full-access socket clients authenticate with a JSON preamble before MCP transport starts; restricted sockets stay unchanged.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Fable 007 authenticated full-tool socket mode.
- Source plan step: 3.
- Preserve: `runSocketConnection(fd:)` stays byte-for-byte unchanged.

## Success criteria
- Add `runFullAccessSocketConnection(fd:expectedToken:)` beside the restricted handler.
- Route listener fds to it only when `exposeAllTools` is true.
- Auth preamble is exactly `{"rpce_auth":{"token":"<value>"}}\n`.
- Read the auth line byte by byte, capped at 4096 bytes, with no JSON-RPC over-read.
- Enforce a 10-second deadline; timeout closes the fd and serves no tools.
- Accepted auth writes `{"rpce_auth":{"status":"accepted"}}\n` and serves with `discoveryRestricted: false`.
- Rejected, malformed, oversized, or empty-token auth writes `{"rpce_auth":{"status":"rejected"}}\n`, closes, and serves no tools.
- Constant-time UTF-8 token compare guards empty input before modulo indexing.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: auth line decode, constant-time compare, accept/reject writes, listener routing.
- Do not: import crypto, edit `HeadlessToolSchemas.swift`, or alter internal callback/discovery sockets.
- Do not refactor or touch `runSocketConnection(fd:)`; if formatting forces a file-wide diff, preserve its body.
- Do not echo token material in any response or log.

## Failure behavior
- Bad JSON/shape/token, empty token, or oversized line: send the rejection frame and close.
- If `expectedToken` is empty or unavailable when the full-access handler is invoked, fail closed without serving tools and do not send an accepted frame.
- Timeout: close without serving MCP tools; no daemon crash.
- Empty client token must not trap on empty-array indexing.
- Perform any post-auth `StdioTransport` setup that can fail before writing the accepted frame.
- If setup cannot operate after pre-read, close the fd, emit only a non-sensitive diagnostic or thrown error path, serve no tools, and do not send an accepted frame.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- Runtime surface: fds from `HeadlessUnixSocketListener`.
- MCP surface: `HeadlessToolSchemas.tools` after auth; discovery tools otherwise.
- Likely files:
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessMCPServer.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/main.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/UnixSocketListener.swift` for fd ownership context only.

## Validation
Commands:
```bash
make dev-swift-build PRODUCT=rpce-headless
grep -n "func runSocketConnection" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessMCPServer.swift
grep -n "discoveryRestricted: false" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessMCPServer.swift
grep -n "RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN\|rpce_auth\|expectedToken" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessMCPServer.swift Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/main.swift
git diff --word-diff=plain -- Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessMCPServer.swift
```

Expected:
- Build exits 0.
- Grep shows full access isolated to the new handler and pre-existing stdio path.
- Security grep summary shows token material is parsed/compared only and never logged or echoed.
- Focused diff note confirms `runSocketConnection(fd:)` remains byte-for-byte unchanged.

Minimal probe:
- Add or run a protocol-level probe in this bead that starts `serve --socket --expose-all-tools` with a test token.
- Verify accepted and rejected `rpce_auth` frames.
- Confirm no JSON-RPC bytes are consumed before auth.

Deferred to downstream smoke:
- Full accepted/rejected/no-handshake/restricted-control smoke matrix remains owned by the downstream smoke bead.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on CLI startup gate.
- Unblocks bridge auth and socket smoke coverage.
- Single-owner risk: `HeadlessMCPServer.swift` fd lifecycle and auth pre-read.

## Closure evidence
Close with: build and minimal protocol evidence, byte-for-byte restricted-handler diff note, security grep summary, commit SHA or uncommitted note.

title: Fable 007.4: cover socket auth and regressions
description:
## Outcome
A dedicated smoke script proves the authenticated full-tool socket works and that restricted/stdio behavior did not regress.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Fable 007 authenticated full-tool socket mode.
- Source plan step: 5 and test plan.
- Preserves: smoke uses direct Unix sockets plus the bridge path, not a white-box shortcut.

## Success criteria
- Create `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/socket_auth_smoke.py`.
- Script starts full-tool socket server with fake HOME env pattern matching `mcp_agent_smoke.py`.
- CLI gate: `serve --root . --expose-all-tools` exits 64 with the planned `--expose-all-tools requires --socket` message.
- CLI gate: missing/blank `RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN` with `--socket --expose-all-tools` exits 64 with the startup-gate message fragments from Fable 007.1:
  - fragments are sufficient; Fable 007.1 owns the canonical diagnostic wording;
  - mentions `RPCE_SOCKET_AUTH_TOKEN`;
  - says the token is missing/blank or shorter than 16 characters;
  - never includes the token value.
- Correct token: handshake accepted; `initialize` + `tools/list` shows all 11 tools including agent/context/oracle tools.
- Wrong token: rejected and server closes the connection.
- Empty token: rejected, connection closes, and the daemon survives a later correct-token connection.
- No handshake: initial JSON-RPC request does not receive served tools and closes/rejects within a 12-second timeout budget; the script may allow a small subprocess cleanup grace period after that assertion.
- Restricted control server without `--expose-all-tools`: no token needed; exactly 7 discovery tools; `agent_run` absent.
- Bridge path: `connect --auth` speaks clean JSON-RPC over stdio and does not leak auth frames to stdout.
- Script prints exactly `SOCKET AUTH SMOKE OK` on success.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: black-box socket/bridge smoke, CLI startup-gate checks, and targeted regression assertions.
- Do not: rely on external services, real agent credentials, network, or app launch.
- Do not weaken existing `mcp_smoke.py` or `mcp_agent_smoke.py` expectations.

## Failure behavior
- Assertion failures should identify the scenario and observed response enough for quick triage.
- Server subprocesses and socket files are cleaned up even on failure where practical.
- If a scenario fails twice after a reasonable fix attempt, treat the plan STOP condition as active.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- Existing helper style:
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/mcp_agent_smoke.py`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/mcp_smoke.py`
- Runtime surface: `socket.socket(socket.AF_UNIX)` and subprocess bridge stdio.
- CLI surface: invalid `serve --expose-all-tools` startup invocations.
- Tool names to assert: discovery tools vs `agent_run`, `agent_manage`, `context_builder`, `oracle_send`.

## Validation
```bash
make dev-swift-build PRODUCT=rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/socket_auth_smoke.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/mcp_smoke.py .build/debug/rpce-headless "$PWD"
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/mcp_agent_smoke.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
```
Expected: `SOCKET AUTH SMOKE OK`, `ALL OK`, and `AGENT MCP SMOKE OK`.

Behavior-test intent: this bead owns the black-box proof that CLI gating, auth, crash regression, restricted sockets, bridge auth, and stdio regressions all hold.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on CLI startup gate, socket protocol, and bridge auth.
- Single-owner risk: smoke helper conventions and subprocess cleanup.

## Closure evidence
Close with: smoke outputs, regression outputs, invalid-startup exit/message evidence, any skipped scenario rationale, commit SHA or uncommitted note.

title: Fable 008.2: route agent_run through pid/reaper ownership
description:
## Outcome
`agent_run.start` sessions are owned by pid plus a reaper task instead of `Foundation.Process`, while preserving output capture and spawn-failure cleanup.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Fable 008 process lifecycle closure.
- Depends on: headless process-group spawn/reap helper.
- Source plan step: 2.

## Success criteria
- `SessionRecord` no longer stores `Process`; it keeps `processID` and adds a stored `reaperTask`.
- `startRun` keeps the existing `Pipe` read handlers for stdout/stderr capture.
- Launch uses `HeadlessProcessGroupLauncher.spawn` with `AgentLauncher.render` argv/environment.
- Parent write ends are closed after successful spawn so read handlers can observe EOF.
- A detached reaper task calls `reapExitCode(pid:)` exactly once and then `completeSession`.
- Spawn failure keeps existing cleanup: nil handlers, remove session record, remove temp dir, rethrow.
- Old `/usr/bin/env` fallback and post-run `setProcessGroup(for:)` are removed from this path.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: session record pid/reaper ownership and `agent_run.start` launch wiring.
- Do not: change cancellation semantics yet; `cancel` may still be repaired by the downstream cancelling bead.
- Do not: change context-builder launch in this bead.
- Do not create duplicate `processID`; reuse the existing field.

## Failure behavior
- Spawn failures leave no leaked session record or temp directory.
- Reaper is the only code path that waits on the child pid.
- Pipe write-end closing must not lose output; existing happy-path smoke output remains visible.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- User-visible surface: MCP `agent_run` ops `start`, `poll`, `wait`.
- Likely file: `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessAgentSessionManager.swift`.
- Current anchors:
  - `SessionRecord` stores `process`, `stdoutPipe`, `stderrPipe`, `processID`.
  - `startRun` configures `Process`, handlers, `terminationHandler`, `process.run()`, and `setProcessGroup`.
  - `mcp_agent_smoke.py` proves fake-agent start/list/get_log/cleanup happy path.

## Validation

```bash
make dev-swift-build PRODUCT=rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/mcp_agent_smoke.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
```
Expected: build exits 0 and smoke prints `AGENT MCP SMOKE OK`.

Behavior-test intent: preserve existing happy-path agent session behavior; new lifecycle edge cases remain deferred to plan 011.

## Dependency / parallel notes

- Unblocks truthful `cancelling` state work.
- Single-owner risk: `HeadlessAgentSessionManager.swift` session-record shape and pipe ownership.

## Closure evidence

title: Fable 008.3: expose truthful cancelling state
description:
## Outcome
Headless agent sessions expose a truthful non-terminal `cancelling` state through snapshots, summaries, and `agent_manage` state filters.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Fable 008 process lifecycle closure.
- Depends on: `agent_run` pid/reaper ownership.
- Source plan steps: 3 and 4.

## Success criteria
- `HeadlessAgentRunStatus` includes raw value `cancelling`.
- `isTerminal` returns false for `running` and `cancelling`, true for completed/failed/cancelled/expired.
- `cancelRun` and `stopSession` return snapshots/summaries with state `cancelling` after requesting termination.
- Repeat cancel while already cancelling is safe and idempotent because `cancelling` is non-terminal.
- `completeSession` maps running/cancelling records to final completed/failed/cancelled only after reaper exit.
- `statusText(for:)` is exhaustive and includes a clear cancelling message.
- `agent_manage.list_sessions` schema filter enum includes `cancelling`.
- Discovery-restricted sockets still do not expose process-backed agent tools.
- Decide and implement summary liveness explicitly: `is_live` should be true for non-terminal `running` and `cancelling` sessions unless implementation finds a stronger existing contract.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: status enum, terminal contract, cancel/stop state transition, snapshot/status text, list-session filter schema.
- Do not: change termination escalation internals, pipe closing, or output truncation in this bead.
- Do not: touch socket-mode discovery restrictions except preserving schema-test expectations.
- Do not add new Python lifecycle tests; plan 011 owns cancellation behavior coverage.

## Failure behavior
- Terminal sessions still reject cancel through the existing terminal guard.
- Cancelling sessions are not eligible for `cleanup_sessions` because they are non-terminal.
- Wait after cancel must keep looping until reaper-driven terminal state.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- User-visible MCP surfaces:
  - `agent_run.cancel`, `agent_run.wait`, `agent_manage.stop_session`, `agent_manage.list_sessions`.
- Data/state anchors:
  - `HeadlessAgentRunStatus` raw values and `isTerminal`.
  - `HeadlessAgentRunSnapshot.status/status_text`.
  - `HeadlessAgentSessionSummary.state/is_live`.
  - `HeadlessToolSchemas.swift` `agent_manage` state enum.
- Existing schema coverage:
  - `Tests/RepoPromptTests/MCP/HeadlessAgentToolSchemaTests.swift`.

## Validation
```bash
make dev-swift-build PRODUCT=rpce-headless
make dev-test FILTER=HeadlessAgentToolSchemaTests
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/mcp_agent_smoke.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
rg -n "case cancelling" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessAgentTypes.swift
```
Expected: build and focused schema test exit 0; smoke prints `AGENT MCP SMOKE OK`; grep shows exactly one `case cancelling`.

Behavior-test intent: keep happy path and schema contract green now; plan 011 should add direct cancel-to-cancelled and cleanup-skip-cancelling tests.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on `agent_run` pid/reaper integration because final terminal state must come from reaping.
- Unblocks termination/escalation/fd/truncation cleanup.
- Single-owner risk: public status strings and schema enum compatibility.

## Closure evidence
Close with: build/smoke/schema/grep outputs, stated `is_live` decision, schema enum evidence, and commit SHA or uncommitted note.

title: Fable 008.5: route context_builder through spawn helper
description:
## Outcome
- `context_builder` discovery-agent execution uses the shared process-group spawn/reap helper.
- The post-`run()` group race is removed while prefixed output and timeout behavior stay intact.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Fable 008 process lifecycle closure.
- Depends on: completed session-manager termination cleanup in the serialized lane.
- Source plan step: 7.

## Success criteria
- `runAgent` launches `RenderedAgentLaunch.argv/environment` through `HeadlessProcessGroupLauncher.spawn`.
- stdout/stderr still flow through `prefixPipe(..., label: "agent|")`.
- Parent write ends close after spawn so prefixed output reaches EOF.
- The returned code comes from `reapExitCode(pid:)`, not `Process.waitUntilExit()`.
- Timeout signals `-pid` and `pid` with SIGTERM.
- Timeout escalates with SIGKILL after 2 seconds if `kill(pid, 0)` still reports liveness.
- Bare-command PATH lookup is preserved by `posix_spawnp`; remove `/usr/bin/env` indirection here.
- Discovery-restricted nested socket behavior remains unchanged.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: context-builder `runAgent` launch/wait/timeout wiring only.
- Do not: change `AgentLauncher.render`, prompt generation, MCP config shape, oracle behavior, or socket filtering.
- Do not: change `agent_run` except for compile integration from landed upstream beads.
- Do not add new lifecycle Python coverage; plan 011 owns those tests.

## Failure behavior
- Spawn failures use the helper error path and include command/errno.
- Timeout escalation must not reap; the detached reap path owns `waitpid`.
- Pipe handlers are nilled/closed best-effort after completion so no EOF hang remains.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- User-visible surfaces: MCP `context_builder` and CLI context-build path.
- Likely file: `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessContextBuilderService.swift`.
- Current anchor: `runAgent` has `Process`, `setpgid`, `waitUntilExit`, and `process.isRunning` timeout checks.
- Smoke sentinels: `CONTEXT_BUILDER_MCP OK`, `CONTEXT_BUILD OK`, `AGENT MCP SMOKE OK`.

## Validation
```bash
make dev-swift-build PRODUCT=rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/context_builder_mcp_fake_agent_test.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/context_build_fake_agent_test.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/mcp_agent_smoke.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
rg -n "setpgid" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer
```
Expected: build exits 0; smokes print the three OK sentinels; `setpgid` matches only the helper if at all.

Behavior-test intent: existing context smokes prove this lane; plan 011 owns timeout/missing-binary lifecycle tests.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on `repoprompt-ce-fable-008-termination-cleanup-i27`.
- Unblocks final closeout bead `repoprompt-ce-fable-008-drift-check-2p6`.
- Keep this serialized with other headless lifecycle edits/builds.
- Single-owner risk: context-builder process lifecycle and timeout behavior.

## Closure evidence
Close with: build/smoke outputs, `setpgid` grep result, timeout summary, and commit SHA or uncommitted note.

title: Fable 008.6: close lane validation and plan status
description:

title: Fable 008: close headless agent process lifecycle
description:
## Outcome
The lane makes headless process-backed agents lifecycle-truthful:
- `agent_run` and `context_builder` children start in process groups created at spawn time.
- Cancellation remains non-terminal until the child pid is reaped.
- Terminal sessions close pipe handles instead of waiting for client cleanup.

## Background
- Source plan: `docs/plans/fable/008-agent-process-lifecycle.md`.
- Current `Foundation.Process` launch calls `setpgid` after `run()`, so group setup can race with exec.
- Current `cancel` and `stop_session` report `.cancelled` before the process tree exits.
- Premature terminal state lets `cleanup_sessions` remove records while descendants may still run.

## Final decisions to preserve
- Run the plan drift/current-state guard before lifecycle edits and STOP on mismatch.
- Add a headless-only helper in `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer`.
- Do not import app-target process infrastructure.
- Use `posix_spawnp` with `POSIX_SPAWN_SETPGROUP`.
- Keep stdin as `/dev/null`.
- Add raw status `cancelling`; only `running` and `cancelling` are non-terminal.
- `cancel` and `stop_session` return `cancelling` immediately.
- `wait` observes final `cancelled` only after `waitpid` reaps.
- Keep terminal session retention client-driven via `cleanup_sessions`.
- Do not add Plan 011 lifecycle Python coverage in this lane.

## Closure contract
Do not close this parent until every direct child is closed, or explicitly closed as unnecessary with evidence. This parent is a PR/lane closure gate, not an implementation task.

## Children / intended order
1. `repoprompt-ce-fable-008-spawn-reap-helper-x1h` — run drift guard, then create the shared process-group spawn/reap primitive.
2. `repoprompt-ce-fable-008-agent-run-pid-reaper-73a` — route `agent_run` through pid/reaper ownership after the helper.
3. `repoprompt-ce-fable-008-cancelling-state-9mo` — expose non-terminal `cancelling` after final state comes from reaping.
4. `repoprompt-ce-fable-008-termination-cleanup-i27` — bound escalation, fd cleanup, and output truncation after the cancelling contract.
5. `repoprompt-ce-fable-008-context-builder-spawn-x9u` — route context-builder discovery-agent execution through the helper after session-manager lifecycle semantics are settled.
6. `repoprompt-ce-fable-008-drift-check-2p6` — final closeout validation, README status row, scope check, and parent evidence.

## Scope
- In-scope files only:
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessProcessGroupLauncher.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessAgentSessionManager.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessAgentTypes.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessContextBuilderService.swift`
  - `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessToolSchemas.swift`
  - `docs/plans/fable/README.md`

## Non-goals
- No changes under `Sources/RepoPrompt/Infrastructure/Process/*` except reading exemplars.
- No new Python lifecycle tests.
- No auto-expiry or retention sweep for terminal sessions.
- No stdin steering/respond support.
- No socket-mode discovery restriction changes.

## Parent acceptance criteria
- Direct child beads are closed with validation evidence.
- Drift guard explicitly confirms the plan excerpts or stops the lane.
- `docs/plans/fable/README.md` marks plan 008 done only after implementation validation.
- Scope check shows no lane-authored files outside the in-scope list.
- `cleanup_sessions` cannot delete a cancelling session because `cancelling.isTerminal == false`.
- `setpgid` and `terminationHandler` are gone from the old call sites.

## Validation
```bash
br dep cycles --json
bv --robot-insights
bv --robot-plan
git status --short
make dev-swift-build PRODUCT=rpce-headless
make dev-test FILTER=HeadlessAgentToolSchemaTests
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/mcp_agent_smoke.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/context_builder_mcp_fake_agent_test.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/context_build_fake_agent_test.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
rg -n "setpgid" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer
rg -n "terminationHandler" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessAgentSessionManager.swift
rg -n "case cancelling" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessAgentTypes.swift
make dev-format-check
```
Expected:
- Build exits 0.
- Schema test exits 0 and discovery-restricted sockets still omit agent/oracle/context tools.
- Smokes print `AGENT MCP SMOKE OK`, `CONTEXT_BUILDER_MCP OK`, and `CONTEXT_BUILD OK`.
- Grep matches only the planned helper for group setup.
- Session-manager `terminationHandler` grep has no matches.
- `case cancelling` grep has exactly one match.

## Parallelization notes
- Treat all children as a serialized lane on one worktree/build lane.
- Only `repoprompt-ce-fable-008-spawn-reap-helper-x1h` is ready at lane start.
- Do not run context-builder work beside other headless edits; it depends on the termination cleanup bead.
- Before any commit, run `.agents/skills/rpce-contribution-check/scripts/preflight.sh commit` after staging.
- Before any push, run the same preflight in push mode.

## Closure evidence
Close with:
- child bead IDs and closure evidence summary;
- final validation commands and sentinel outputs;
- final grep and scope-check results;
- commit SHA or note that changes remain uncommitted;
- deferred follow-ups, especially Plan 011 lifecycle tests.

title: Fable 008.1: add headless process-group spawn/reap helper
description:
## Outcome
A headless-only helper spawns children in their own process group and provides the single waitpid reaper used by later headless agent paths.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Fable 008 process lifecycle closure.
- Source plan: `docs/plans/fable/008-agent-process-lifecycle.md`.
- Preserves: app-target process infrastructure is exemplar-only.

## Success criteria
- Run the plan drift check before editing; STOP if current-state excerpts mismatch.
- Add `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessProcessGroupLauncher.swift`.
- `spawn(...)` uses `posix_spawnp` and `POSIX_SPAWN_SETPGROUP` so PATH lookup works and pgid equals child pid from spawn time.
- Stdin is `/dev/null`; stdout/stderr are dup2'd to caller pipe write fds.
- Darwin-only `POSIX_SPAWN_CLOEXEC_DEFAULT` is guarded; Glibc imports mirror `UnixSocketListener.swift`.
- Spawn failures throw `HeadlessToolFailure` with command, errno, and system text.
- `reapExitCode(pid:)` retries `waitpid` on `EINTR` and returns exit status or `128 + signal`.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: drift guard, POSIX file actions/attrs, C-string argv/envp handling, spawn errors, and reaper helper.
- Do not: wire session-manager or context-builder call sites here.
- Do not: import `Sources/RepoPrompt/Infrastructure/Process`.
- Do not: add lifecycle Python coverage; Plan 011 owns kill-tree tests.

## Failure behavior
- Drift mismatch blocks the lane before lifecycle edits.
- Empty `argv` or setup failure fails before spawn with a clear `HeadlessToolFailure`.
- Missing binary reports command and errno to MCP callers.
- Unexpected wait failure returns a nonzero failure-style code and avoids a second reap path.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- New file: `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessProcessGroupLauncher.swift`.
- Drift targets: session manager, agent types, and context-builder service under `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/`.
- Pattern sources: `UnixSocketListener.swift`, app-target `ProcessLauncher.swift`, and app-target `ProcessTermination.swift`.

## Validation
```bash
git status --short
git diff --stat 0bd2270..HEAD -- \
  Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessAgentSessionManager.swift \
  Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessAgentTypes.swift \
  Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessContextBuilderService.swift
make dev-swift-build PRODUCT=rpce-headless
```
Expected: drift is reconciled with plan excerpts before editing; build exits 0.

Behavior-test intent: downstream integration beads prove the helper through existing black-box smokes.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- This is the only ready frontier for Fable 008 at lane start.
- Unblocks `repoprompt-ce-fable-008-agent-run-pid-reaper-73a`.
- Single-owner risk: POSIX compile portability across Darwin/Glibc.

## Closure evidence
Close with: drift output, excerpt-match confirmation, build result, helper behavior summary, portability caveat, and commit SHA or uncommitted note.

title: Fable 008.4: bound termination cleanup and output capture
description:
## Outcome
Termination, escalation, terminal pipe cleanup, and output truncation are bounded and truthful for `agent_run` sessions.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Fable 008 process lifecycle closure.
- Depends on: `cancelling` state contract.
- Source plan steps: 5 and 6.

## Success criteria
- `terminate(record:)` uses `record.processID`, not `Process`.
- Termination sends SIGTERM to process group and direct pid.
- A stored escalation task sends SIGKILL after 2 seconds if the child remains alive.
- Repeat cancel/stop while cancelling is idempotent and does not spawn unbounded escalation tasks.
- `completeSession` cancels escalation, drains pipes, nils handlers, closes read handles, records exit, and sets terminal status.
- `cleanupSessions` tolerates already-closed read handles and removes only terminal sessions.
- stdout/stderr capture truncates by UTF-8 bytes, not Swift `Character` count.
- Add a narrow comment for replacement characters when byte truncation splits a multi-byte sequence.
- `shutdown()` requests termination for non-terminal sessions through the record-based path.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: signaling, escalation tracking, fd cleanup, cleanup tolerance, and byte-accurate output caps.
- Do not: add retention sweeps or automatic cleanup of terminal session records.
- Do not: reap from timeout/escalation tasks; the reaper task remains the only waitpid owner.
- Do not add plan 011 lifecycle tests here.

## Failure behavior
- Missing or invalid pid makes termination a no-op without crashing.
- Group SIGTERM failure logs only unexpected errno; ESRCH is tolerated.
- FD close is best-effort and must not prevent final status from being recorded.
- Output capture never exceeds the configured byte budget after this bead.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- Likely file: `Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessAgentSessionManager.swift`.
- Current anchors:
  - `shutdown()` terminates non-terminal `record.process`.
  - `cancelRun` and `stopSession` request termination.
  - `completeSession` drains pipes and nils handlers.
  - `cleanupSessions` removes terminal records/temp dirs.
  - `append` computes byte counts but truncates by `text.prefix(allowed)`.

## Validation
```bash
make dev-swift-build PRODUCT=rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/mcp_agent_smoke.py .build/debug/rpce-headless
rg -n "terminationHandler" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessAgentSessionManager.swift
```
Expected:
- Build exits 0.
- Smoke prints `AGENT MCP SMOKE OK`.
- Grep produces no matches.

Behavior-test intent: plan 011 owns kill-tree, timeout escalation, and cancelling-to-cancelled tests. Do not weaken this bead because those tests are deferred.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on the cancelling state contract.
- Single-owner risk: `HeadlessAgentSessionManager.swift` lifecycle paths and pipe handles.
- Avoid concurrent edits to the same manager file unless agents explicitly reserve regions.

## Closure evidence
Close with: build/smoke/grep outputs, tracked-escalation note, reaper-only waitpid note, byte-truncation evidence, and commit SHA or uncommitted note.

title: Fable 009: gate context-builder oracle follow-up
description:
## Outcome
Gate headless context_builder oracle follow-up so it runs only after discovery exits 0 and selects at least one file.

## Source / decisions
- Plan: docs/plans/fable/009-context-builder-oracle-gating.md
- Lane: fable-009; baseline: 0bd2270; direct S bug bead.
- Preserve: review still calls oracle after successful non-empty discovery.
- No schema/tool result, OracleService, OpenAICompatibleClient, DiscoverPromptBuilder, or token-budget changes.
- Independent of 007/008; serialize with other rpce-headless target edits.

## Success criteria
- question/plan/review skip oracle on nonzero agentExit or empty selectedFiles.
- Failed discovery reports agent_failed with no answer/follow_up_hint and no missing-key oracle error.
- Empty successful discovery reports empty_selection with no answer/follow_up_hint.
- Successful non-empty question/plan/review still calls oracle.
- selection/clarify remains oracle-free and fake-agent happy path stays completed.
- CLI context-build shuts down OracleService on success and throw paths.
- Oracle message includes response mode/instruction because send has no mode parameter.
- Plan 009 row in docs/plans/fable/README.md is updated on completion.

## Scope / non-goals
- Touch only context builder service, context-build command, two fake-agent smoke scripts, and the plan README row.
- Do not change result fields, schemas, socket auth, cancellation, lifecycle, or unrelated docs.

## Split decision
- Behavior atoms stay together in this bead:
  - failed/empty-discovery gating;
  - successful non-empty question/plan/review oracle follow-up;
  - selection/clarify oracle-free behavior;
  - agent_failed/empty_selection responses without answer/follow_up_hint;
  - CLI OracleService shutdown;
  - response-mode oracle instruction;
  - fake-agent smokes and README closeout.
- These are one reviewable S-sized sub-plan outcome because they prove the same context_builder oracle-follow-up contract across MCP and CLI surfaces.
- Do not split unless drift shows CLI shutdown or oracle-mode messaging moved into a separate owner/surface.

## Failure behavior / STOP
- Discovery errors and exit codes stay visible to MCP/CLI callers.
- Run drift check first; if status derivation or oracle call site no longer matches the plan, stop.
- If instructions-only empty-selection Q&A appears legitimate, stop and ask before loosening the gate.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- MCP: status, plan/review answer fields, follow_up_hint, agent_exit, selection.
- CLI: context-build stdout plus propagated exit code.
- Code: HeadlessContextBuilderExecution.status, run(request:oracleService:), runOracleFollowUpIfNeeded(...), ContextBuildCommand.run().
- Smokes: context_builder_mcp_fake_agent_test.py and context_build_fake_agent_test.py.

## Validation
```bash
git diff --stat 0bd2270..HEAD -- Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessContextBuilderService.swift Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/ContextBuildCommand.swift
make dev-swift-build PRODUCT=rpce-headless
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/context_builder_mcp_fake_agent_test.py .build/debug/rpce-headless "$PWD"
python3 Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/context_build_fake_agent_test.py .build/debug/rpce-headless "$PWD"
rg -n "runOracleFollowUpIfNeeded|agentExit|selectedFiles" Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessContextBuilderService.swift
git status --short
```
Expected:
- build exits 0.
- smokes print CONTEXT_BUILDER_MCP OK and CONTEXT_BUILD OK.
- no-key smoke assertions prove failed/empty discovery skips oracle.
- rg is only a drift/anchor inspection aid for the oracle follow-up call site, not the closure proof or an implementation-shape requirement.

## Regression test intent
- MCP: failed fake agent, response_type question, oracle keys removed, assert agent_failed and no answer/follow-up.
- CLI: FAKE_AGENT_EXIT=3, --response-type question, oracle keys removed, assert exit 3 and absence of answer output.
- Keep existing clarify/selection coverage.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on: none. This bead is the ready frontier.
- Do not implement concurrently with active edits to:
  - Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/HeadlessContextBuilderService.swift
  - Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/ContextBuildCommand.swift
  - Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/context_builder_mcp_fake_agent_test.py
  - Sources/RepoPromptHeadlessServer/Scripts/context_build_fake_agent_test.py
- Unrelated 007/008 work can proceed only if it does not touch those files.

## Closure evidence
Close with behavior summary, commands/results, no-key skip proof, commit SHA or uncommitted note, and any follow-up bead.
